project_name: Artemisinin Biosynthesis Pathway Reconstruction and Optimization

edges.audit_trail: Page 3, 'The first committed and limiting step of artemisinin biosynthesis should be the cyclization of FPP by ADS.'
Page 4, 'CYP71AV1 was... shown to catalyze the oxidation of the amorpha-4,11-diene, artemisinic alcohol...'
Page 4, 'CYP71AV1... shown to catalyze the oxidation of the... artemisinic alcohol'
Page 4, 'Dbr2 was cloned and characterized, shown to be relatively specific for artemisinic aldehyde'
Page 4, Figure 3; 'Aldh1... capable of catalyzing the oxidation artemisinic aldehyde'
Page 4, 'Aldh1... capable of catalyzing the oxidation artemisinic aldehyde, dihydroartemisinic aldehyde'
Page 5, 'in vivo dihydroartemisinic acid undergoes rapid plant pigment photosensitized oxidation, followed by subsequent spontaneous oxidation to form artemisinin.'
Page 3, Figure 2; 'The cDNA encoding sesquiterpene synthase firstly cloned belongs to the epi-cedol synthase (ECS)'
Page 3, 'Since squalene synthase (SS) is responsible for sterol biosynthesis, competitive with FPP, it is considered as a regulatory switch.'

risk_report.reason: Squalene synthase competes directly with ADS for the universal precursor FPP. Sterols reduce the total carbon flux available for artemisinin.
risk_report.audit_trail: Page 3, Paragraph 4: 'Since squalene synthase (SS) is responsible for sterol biosynthesis, competitive with FPP...'

risk_report.reason: Endogenous sesquiterpene synthases structurally parallel to ADS cyclize FPP into unwanted volatile/terpene byproducts.
risk_report.audit_trail: Page 3, Figure 2: 'Reactions catalyzed by the four sesquiterpene synthases cloned from A. annua'

risk_report.reason: Artemisinic acid is converted to Arteannuin B in competing branches. This indicates an incomplete or misdirected cyclization/oxidation sequence away from artemisinin.
risk_report.audit_trail: Page 4, Other possible intermediates: 'Artemisinic acid is a common precursor of arteannuin B... Page 5: possible non-enzymatically into several compounds including arteannuin B'

yield_optimization_strategies.description: Introduce antisense SS cDNA to down-regulate Squalene Synthase (SS) mRNA. This reduces sterol biosynthesis and tremendously elevates mRNA coding for amorpha-4,11-diene synthase (ADS), pushing FPP flux into the artemisinin pathway.
yield_optimization_strategies.audit_trail: Page 3, Paragraph 4: 'An antisense SS cDNA was introduced... resulting in the decline of SS mRNA and simultaneous reduction of total sterols, mRNA coding for amorpha-4,11-diene synthase tremendously elevated.'

yield_optimization_strategies.description: Co-express Farnesyl Pyrophosphate Synthase (FPPS) and 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl CoA reductase (HMGR) to increase the upstream FPP pool. Additionally, co-express Cytochrome P450 Reductase (CPR) with CYP71AV1 to prevent electron-transfer bottlenecks during the multi-step oxidation of amorpha-4,11-diene.
yield_optimization_strategies.audit_trail: Page 2: 'Genes of HMGR and FPPS were co-expressed... inducing 2.32-fold content increase'. Page 4: 'CPR... has been cloned and co-expressed with CYP71AV1 and ADS... leading to high levels of artemisinic acid.'

yield_optimization_strategies.description: Apply abiotic stress such as cold acclimation (pre-chilling), heat shock, or UV light exposure to living cultures or plants. These stresses naturally up-regulate the transcription levels of both ADS and CYP71AV1 (up to 7 to 10-fold).
yield_optimization_strategies.audit_trail: Page 3: 'upon exposure to cold, heat shock or UV light, the transcription levels of ADS were up-regulated'. Page 4: 'CYP71AV1 genes is up-regulated to 10- and 7-fold... after being induced by low temperature stress.'
